chief_complaint: An elderly male patient with Stage IV lung adenocarcinoma presented with severe coma, hyperglycemia, and metabolic disorder.
history: The patient was diagnosed with Stage IV lung adenocarcinoma harboring KRAS p.G12C and TP53 p.R273C mutations. He achieved sustained stable disease and symptomatic improvement through a sequential therapeutic strategy, including platinum-based chemotherapy followed by the PD-1 inhibitor sintilimab combined with anti-angiogenic agents (apatinib or anlotinib).
exam: Vital signs: severe hyperglycemia, metabolic disorder. Physical examination: coma.
labs: Laboratory investigations confirmed fulminant ICI-related diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA).
differentials: Diabetic ketoacidosis due to underlying diabetes mellitus
correct_ranking: Diabetic ketoacidosis due to underlying diabetes mellitus
